2606.22454.title: CASPER in the Machine: Insights into Character Variety in LLM-Generated Stories
2606.22454.paper_summary: As LLM-generated text is increasingly used, especially in fictional domains, we explore how much LLM-generated stories differ from human-written stories. In this work, we focus on characters. We borrow definitions from narratology to analyze eight intricate dimensions of character, such as stylization and wholeness. These dimensions consider more than just basic characteristics. They assess how characters are portrayed within their stories. After automatically inferring categories of characters within both LLM and human-written stories, we compare and contrast these two sets of stories. We consider the following overarching questions: (1) Do LLMs and human-written stories have similar characters? and (2) Do LLMs generate stories with a variety of characters? Our analysis includes research questions that focus on stories generated by popular LLMs and recently published human-written stories. We describe a number of interesting similarities, differences and key takeaways.
2606.22454.paper_summary_zh: 隨著LLM生成的文本在虛構領域中的使用越來越普遍，我們探討LLM生成的故事與人類撰寫的故事之間的差異有多大。在這項工作中，我們專注於角色。我們借用敘事學的定義來分析角色的八個複雜維度，例如風格化和整體性。這些維度考慮的不僅僅是基本特徵。它們評估角色在故事中的描繪方式。在自動推斷LLM和人類撰寫故事中的角色類別後，我們比較和對比這兩組故事。我們考慮以下幾個總體問題：(1) LLM和人類撰寫的故事是否有相似的角色？以及(2) LLM生成的故事是否具有多樣的角色？我們的分析包括專注於流行LLM生成的故事和最近發表的人類撰寫故事的研究問題。我們描述了一些有趣的相似之處、差異和關鍵要點。
2606.22454.author: Anneliese Brei et.al.
2606.22454.authors: Anneliese Brei, Abhisheik Sharma, Nicholas Sanaie, Lu Wang, Snigdha Chaturvedi
